title: Create TValueGeneratorRegistry with bundled heuristics and fallbacks
blocks: Dummy api skeleton â€” the skeleton stores a registry instance and invokes it for every generated field.
Probing map task â€” the probing map registers placeholder generators into the registry for unresolved fields.

status_instructions.on_followup: If you discover a subtle priority/caching behaviour downstream tasks must respect (e.g. case-insensitive name matching), append it to the relevant task's `side_context`.

description: Create `lib/generators/t_value_generator_registry.dart` housing the `TValueGeneratorRegistry` class. The registry owns three internal maps â€” field-name generators, runtime-type generators, and per-field placeholder generators for unresolved schema fields â€” plus the priority-walk `generate` method that every dummy document generation path routes through. The registry also ships with a bundled set of realistic field-name heuristics and bundled runtime-type fallbacks covering String, int, double, num, bool, DateTime, Timestamp, List, Map, GeoPoint, DocumentReference, and Blob.
context_for_junior: Given a field and its type, the registry answers one question: 'which fake value do I return?' It does so by walking a strict priority order so the caller's own overrides always win, then shared name heuristics (so every field called `email` gets an email address), then a type-based fallback, then a last-resort visible placeholder like `'[dummy]'` or `0`. You do not need to be creative â€” the priority walk is written in the spec under `architecture.value_resolution_priority` and the bundled lists are in `supporting_types.TValueGeneratorRegistry.bundled_field_heuristics` / `bundled_type_fallbacks`. The registry is handed a single `Random` from the dummy api so every value is deterministic per run; you do not construct your own Random inside bundled generators.
why_it_matters: This is the one place where 'what does a dummy field look like?' is decided. Every fake document in every feature goes through this class. If priorities drift or defaults go random in different orders, runs stop being deterministic and demos stop being reproducible.
instructions: Expose a factory constructor `TValueGeneratorRegistry.build({Map<String, TValueGenerator>? fieldGenerators, Map<Type, TValueGenerator>? typeGenerators, required TDummySchema schema, required Random random})` that merges caller-supplied maps on top of the bundled defaults and, for every field listed in `schema.unresolved`, registers a visible placeholder generator.
The registry must hold its Random via injection (constructor parameter) â€” never `Random()` from thin air â€” so the dummy api's determinism stays intact.

Implement `dynamic generate({required String field, required Type type})` walking the priority list: caller-field-exact-name â†’ caller-type â†’ bundled-field-exact-name â†’ bundled-field-substring-match â†’ bundled-type â†’ placeholder. Return the first hit.
Substring-match for field-name heuristics is case-insensitive and matches suffixes like `customerEmail` â†’ `email` only when the suffix is preceded by a camelCase boundary or a separator.

Populate bundled field-name heuristics with every entry listed in the spec (`id`, `uid`, `userId`, `ownerId`, `name`, `firstName`, `lastName`, `fullName`, `displayName`, `username`, `email`, `phone`, `phoneNumber`, `description`, `title`, `summary`, `content`, `body`, `url`, `avatarUrl`, `imageUrl`, `thumbnailUrl`, `address`, `street`, `city`, `country`, `zip`, `postalCode`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `deletedAt`, `timestamp`, `age`, `count`, `quantity`, `price`, `amount`, `rating`, `score`, `isActive`, `isDeleted`, `enabled`). Each heuristic consumes the injected Random for any randomness.

Populate bundled runtime-type fallbacks for String, int, double, num, bool, DateTime, Timestamp (cloud_firestore), List, Map, GeoPoint, DocumentReference, Blob per the spec's `bundled_type_fallbacks`. Use lightweight realistic values â€” no external faker dependency.

The placeholder pool is: String â†’ `'[dummy]'`, int â†’ `0`, double â†’ `0.0`, bool â†’ `false`, DateTime â†’ epoch, Timestamp â†’ epoch Timestamp, List â†’ `<dynamic>[]`, Map â†’ `<String, dynamic>{}`.
Keep the bundled name maps `static const` / `static final` where Dart allows; otherwise build them once in a private `_buildBundledFieldHeuristics(Random)` helper invoked from `build`.
Run `make analyze package=turbo_firestore_api` and the new unit tests.
goals: Deterministic, priority-ordered value resolution for any (field, type) pair.
Bundled defaults cover every Firestore-relevant runtime type.
Placeholders for unresolved fields are visible in the UI without crashing.
non_goals: No async work â€” generators are synchronous.
No integration with the probing map â€” that is the next task.
No declarative spec factories (`oneOf`, `rangeInt`, etc.) â€” those live in a separate file and task.
No per-field nested-schema walking â€” generation is field-at-a-time; the caller (dummy api) walks nested schemas recursively.
acceptance_criteria: `make test package=turbo_firestore_api` passes new unit tests for the priority walk.
Given a caller override for `email`, When `generate(field: 'email', type: String)` is called, Then the caller override is returned and the bundled heuristic is not invoked.
Given no caller override, When `generate(field: 'price', type: double)` is called, Then a value from the bundled `price` heuristic is returned and the bundled `double` fallback is not invoked.
Given a field listed in `schema.unresolved`, When `generate` is called on it, Then the placeholder value is returned.

Given two registry instances built with the same seeded Random and same overrides, When `generate` is called with the same (field, type) pairs in the same order, Then both instances produce byte-identical output.
constraints: No workarounds: if a Type cannot be compared with `==` (which Dart allows for first-class Types), do not dodge with string names; use Type equality directly.
Use only dependencies already declared by turbo_firestore_api (cloud_firestore for Timestamp/GeoPoint/Blob/DocumentReference).
Trailing commas, single quotes, const constructors.
Follow the class-structure convention for member ordering.
tools: RepoPrompt MCP for scanning cloud_firestore types referenced in t_firestore_api.dart
Write + Edit for the new file

testing.rules: No mocks. Inject a seeded `Random(42)` so every test assertion is stable. Test outcomes and priority order, not internal map contents. Do not assert on hardcoded strings like `'[dummy]'` directly â€” check the type and, for the placeholder variant, test that it matches the declared placeholder via reference equality to a constant the implementation exposes internally for tests. Avoid literal string comparisons where semantic checks work.
scenarios: Given caller overrides only, When generate is called for an overridden field, Then the override result flows through.
Given bundled heuristics only, When generate is called for `email`, Then the output contains exactly one `@` character (semantic check).
Given an unresolved field marked by the schema, When generate is called for it, Then the output equals the placeholder value for its runtime type.
Given two builds with the same seed and same inputs, When generate is called N times in the same order, Then both outputs are element-wise equal â€” proving determinism.
Given a field name like `customerEmail` (not exactly in the heuristic map), When generate is called with it, Then the substring match resolves it via the `email` heuristic.
testing.refactor_for_testability: Expose the bundled maps and the placeholder constants via package-private getters if a test needs to compare against them. Do not introduce reflection or mirrors.

quality_bar.maintainability: Bundled maps are declared in one spot per category and easy to extend.
quality_bar.dry: Priority walk is implemented once; no duplicated lookup logic across call sites.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: Registry answers 'which value for this (field, type)?' and nothing else â€” no schema walking, no DTO knowledge.
quality_bar.performance: Map lookups are O(1); substring match runs only after the exact-match path misses and caches nothing because inputs are bounded per document.
quality_bar.abuse_resistance: Caller overrides cannot crash the registry â€” unexpected return types are caller's problem, the registry returns `dynamic` by contract.
